mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) after a major contraction signals a durable labor market recovery. This leads to higher aggregate income and consumption, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving equity prices higher over the subsequent quarter.
candidate_trigger: 3-month moving average of month-over-month PAYEMS change > 0
evidence_refs: fact:US economy exited the Great Recession in June 2009; labor market recovery is a key confirmation signal.
failure_condition: In the 12 months after the trigger condition is met, the 60-day forward returns for the S&P 500 are negative in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: Strong momentum in retail sales (RSAFS) indicates robust consumer spending, which comprises the largest part of the US economy. This directly translates into higher corporate revenues, causing equity markets to price in better-than-expected near-term earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:Consumer spending is approximately two-thirds of US GDP.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between the 3-month percentage change in RSAFS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is less than 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is at the zero lower bound, an increase in the rate of inflation (CPIAUCSL) is a positive signal. It indicates that unconventional monetary policy is successfully averting deflation, boosting nominal GDP and corporate revenues, and reducing the real burden of debt.
evidence_refs: fact:The Federal Reserve has held the target rate at 0-0.25% since December 2008.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%, the correlation between the 6-month change in CPIAUCSL and 60-day forward S&P 500 returns is negative.

mechanism_narrative: An accelerating trend in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects a strengthening in the manufacturing and production sectors of the economy. This is a coincident indicator of broad economic health that supports higher equity valuations through the earnings channel.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production bottomed in June 2009 and has been recovering since.
failure_condition: The 3-month change in INDPRO fails to show a statistically significant positive correlation (p > 0.05) with 20-day forward S&P 500 returns over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of a high but falling unemployment rate (UNRATE) and low inflation (CPIAUCSL) creates a 'Goldilocks' environment for equities. This signals a non-inflationary recovery, allowing the Federal Reserve to maintain an accommodative policy stance for longer, which supports asset prices.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE peaked at 10.0% in Oct 2009, while inflation remains very low.
failure_condition: Instances where UNRATE is above 6.5% and falling are not followed by statistically significant positive 60-day S&P 500 returns compared to baseline.

mechanism_narrative: After a collapse in the housing market, any sustained increase in housing starts (HOUST) from a deeply depressed level is a powerful leading indicator of renewed risk appetite and economic activity. This positive signal drives short-term speculative buying in the equity market.
candidate_trigger: HOUST is below 800 thousand units and its 3-month moving average is rising.
evidence_refs: fact:The 2008 financial crisis originated in the US housing market.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the average 5-day forward S&P 500 return is not positive and statistically different from zero.

mechanism_narrative: In a quantitative easing (QE) regime, weak labor market data (negative PAYEMS surprises) reinforces expectations of continued or expanded Federal Reserve asset purchases. This 'bad news is good news' effect lowers long-term discount rates and boosts equity valuations, overwhelming the negative fundamental signal.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS < 0.25% and month-over-month PAYEMS change is negative.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS is near zero, months with negative PAYEMS growth are followed by negative 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 50% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: The first report of positive quarter-over-quarter real GDP (GDPC1) growth after a NBER-defined recession provides official confirmation that the economic contraction has ended. This reduction in macroeconomic uncertainty boosts investor confidence and drives capital into equities.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 q/q annualized growth > 0 for the first time after at least two consecutive negative quarters.
evidence_refs: fact:Real GDP growth for Q3 2009 was reported as positive, ending the recession's contraction phase.
failure_condition: The 60-day S&P 500 return following the first positive GDP print after a recession is negative.
